name: loom-discovery
description: The loom family's problem-space station: establishes what problem exists, for whom, and whether it's worth your time — before principles/design/spec/code start. Two skills: business-value runs an adversarial worth-it check (Shape Up betting register — GO / NO-GO / NEEDS-MORE-RESEARCH; optional, skippable for personal tools, re-entrant after research; market sizing/GTM/revenue delegates to domain-teams:planning-team, never inline) and user-insights runs evidence-linked needs research (job stories, contexts, today's workarounds) that ends in a user-ratified value commitment — the agent proposes from evidence, the user ratifies, never a self-commit. Artifacts land under docs/loom/discovery/<date>-<slug>/ (user-insights.md, evidence.md, research/, optional business-value.md) so the reasoning trail survives for future drift-prevention and re-understanding. Heavyweight research delegates to research-toolkit:deep-deep-research; light scopes use inline WebSearch. Key-free, agent-portable, no hooks.

interface.shortDescription: Establish the problem, the users, and whether it's worth your time before you build
interface.longDescription: The loom family's problem-space station: business-value runs an adversarial worth-it check (Shape Up betting register — GO / NO-GO / NEEDS-MORE-RESEARCH; optional, skippable for personal tools, re-entrant after research) and user-insights runs evidence-linked needs research (job stories, contexts, today's workarounds) that ends in a user-ratified value commitment. Artifacts land under docs/loom/discovery/<date>-<slug>/ so the reasoning trail survives for future drift-prevention and re-understanding.
defaultPrompt: Is this idea worth my time right now?
Research who has this problem and what they need.
Help me establish the problem before I start designing.
